Kelly Faria 03/25/2020 1.0 Updates based on WM input Kelly Faria 03/26/2020 1.1 Updates based on Technical Review EX16 – LM Message for MHE Load LPN This extension enables the LM application to track the productivity of warehouse workers using Engineered Labor Standards (ELS) while completing truck loading via MHE. This is accomplished through the use of LM team standards where a team is defined by a given divert lane or configurable group of divert lanes . Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE Y MHE Load LPN Labor Message customization RF N Reporting N Other N McLane uses MHE scans to complete the systemic loading of oLPNs onto outbound shipping containers. Workers present in the truck must physically load oLPNs from the conveyor to the truck . McLane requires that users performing this work are tracked in LM on standard. This extension customizes the base MHE Load LPN Labor Message to dynamically populate TEAM_STD_GRP in order to track productivity using base Labor Management T eam S tandards. Assumptions Heel to Toe setting in ‘Additional Facility Configuration’ is set to “Accept Begin Times” Base Team Standards Functionality is utilized in LM Employees indicate to LM which team they are on through either an RF or a kiosk Employees not logged onto an LM team do not receive credit for loading events during that time Employees will not receive credit for events occurring when no users are logged onto the corresponding team There is only 1 shipment loaded per dock door at any moment in time . LPNs that are unloaded are not tracked by this modification LPNs that are loaded via RF Load Trailer and not via conveyable line are not track ed by this modification The start time for the LM event is the time of the MHE divert scan and may not be the time the user physically loads the LPN onto the trailer There is a 1-1 relationship between divert lanes and dock doors Divert lane is received as part of the inbound CONTAINERSTATUS message (MHE Load LPN) All diverts where MHE Load LPN occurs are configured in the custom EX16 system code IDs . The divert values stored in the custom system code IDs match the divert values configured in WMOS exactly. Each divert can be assigned to only one team. However, one team may have mul tiple diverts assigned. Team Standard Group names are limited to 15 characters LM is installed on same schema (WHSE_GEN_PARM.ELS_INSTL = 1) LM Activity code is configured for the transaction (TRAN_MASTER.ELS_ACTVTY_CODE <> NULL) The transaction is configured to be working in one way call mode (TRAN_MASTER.WORKINFO_INTERFACE=1) Dependencies Code Dependencies N/A Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Divert Lane to Team Mapping To allow labor credit to be shared on a team that consists of users loading from more than 1 divert at a time, a custom system code is added to control the mapping between the divert lanes and the LM Team Standard Group. Divert Mapping System Code A custom system code is configured for Diver t Lane to Team Standard Group mapping. A code ID exists for every divert lane, and an Edit Box parameter holds the Team Standard Group that divert is assigned to. This allows for configurable team sizes that span one or more divert lanes. Example System Code Example System Code IDs Example System Code ID Parameters System Code Parameter Validation For this custom EX16 System Code, additional validation is added against the value entered in the Code ID Parameters using the logic below. Upon saving a parameter value: WM validates the entered value against the configured Team Standards Groups (E_TEAM_STD.TEAM_CODE). If the entered value matches a record in E_TEAM_STD, WM saves the system code parameter successfully If the entered value does not match a record in E_TEAM_STD, WM does not allow the user to save the parameter and displays an error message – ‘Entered value does not match a configured Team Standard Group’ Enablement IF C-001-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement) IF C-001-EX16 byte 1 = Y (EX16 extension enablement) THEN the functionality detailed in section 3.2 is enabled ELSE the functionality detailed in section 3.2 is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in section 3.2 is disabled Labor Message The base MHE Load LPN labor message hook is utilized to generate the LABOR_MSG and LABOR_MSG_DTL records when a CONTAINERSTATUS message is received for Loading Divert. The labor message hook is enhanced to populate the divert and team standard group based on the custom logic outlined below . The labor message customizations and logic defined below are enabled if the EX16 transaction parameter is enabled on the MHE Load LPN transaction. 005 – Labor Header Message The base labor header message is customized to include the following fields . If a field is not listed, it is written as per base Load LPN labor message. Field Type Source LOGIN_USER_ID VARCHAR2(50) ‘ TEAMUSER ’ TEAM_STD_GRP VARCHAR2(15) Team Standard Group Name ( based on divert lane and custom system code detailed in section 3. 2.1 ) USER_DEF_VAL_1 VARCHAR2(20) Divert Lane for the current oLPN Logic to Populate Team Standard Group While generating the 005 Labor Header Message, WM evaluates the following logic to determine which value to populate in the TEAM_STD_GRP field: WM compares the divert lane associated to the Labor Message to the Code IDs for custom EX16 System Code and identifies the matching Code ID WM populates the TEAM_STD_GRP field with the corresponding parameter value from the identified Code ID Enablement IF C-001-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement) IF MHE Load LPN t ransaction parameter 500-500 = Y THEN the functionality detailed in section 4.1 is enabled ELSE the functionality detailed in section 4.1 is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in section 4.1 is disabled 010 – Labor Detail Message(s) The base labor detail message(s) are not customized for this extension. UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE No changes to MHE messages in scope for this extension. RF No RF changes in scope for this extension. Process Change/Addition Before an employee begins loading from a given divert , they must log in to the corresponding team to receive credit for loading events . When an employee completes loading from a given divert, or is moving to a different divert , they must log out of the team to end the team assignment . Reporting , Label, or Document Not report, label or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Performance Expectations The p urpose of this section is to identify performance requirements up front Process Step Peak Transaction Rate Max Response 95% Response Recovery Queue Recovery Time Operations Driver & notes Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below. Menu Parameters Parameter (Category > Parameter) Value Warehouse Master Parameter (Tab > Section > Parameter ) Value System Codes Extension Enablement System Codes Code ID Details Parameter Configuration Rec Type Code Type Code ID Short Description Description Frm To Description Validation Type Configured value C 000 000 Global Extension Control Global Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – Global Extension Control is turned ON C 000 EX16 EX16 Extension Control EX16 Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – EX16 Extension Control is turned ON Divert Mapping System Code A code ID is configured for each Divert Lane where the parameter value equals the Team Standard Group that Divert Lane is assigned to. The parameter value is configurable to allow changes to which Divert Lanes are assigned to which Team Standards Groups. Example Code IDs: Code ID Details Parameter Configuration Rec Type Code Type Code ID Short Description Description Frm To Description Validation Type Configured value C X16 DVL01 DVL01 Divert Lane 1 1 15 Team Standard Group Edit Box – Alpha Numeric SHIP TEAM 1 C X16 DVL02 DVL02 Divert Lane 2 1 15 Team Standard Group Edit Box – Alpha Numeric SHIP TEAM 1 C X16 DVL03 DVL03 Divert Lane 3 1 15 Team Standard Group Edit Box – Alpha Numeric SHIP TEAM 2 Database Not required for this extension. Custom Configurations System Code Types Rec Type Code Type Code Desc RF Desc Max Len. Allow Add Allow Delete Allow Parm Maint Allow Desc Mod WHSE/ BU/ Ent Parm Exist Order by C 000 Global Extension Control 4 Y Y Y Y WHSE Y Code Id C EX16 EX16 Controls 4 Y Y Y Y WHSE Y Code Id System Code Parameters Rec Type Code Type From Byte To Byte Parm Desc Parameter Validation C 000 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Program Parameters Program From Byte To Byte Description Parameter Required Parameter Config MHE Load LPN 500 500 EX 16 Enable N Check Box Y/N Flag Test Cases General C onditions Condition Name Description and expectations Exceptions Condition Name Description and expectations Questions and Answers This below table is used to track questions that were asked and answered regarding this enhancement. If the question resulted in any action it is listed against the question. # Asked By Question Answered By Answer Date Added Action Taken 1 2 3 4 Estimates and Acknowledgement All services provided are pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Technology Framework Agreement (“Agreement”) previously entered into between our respective companies on August 27, 2019 and these Agreements provide that such services are billed at the hourly billing rates in effect at the time services are rendered. This acknowledgement is to c over the estimate given below. Please note that the estimate below is good for only 60 days from the date the quote is provided. Description of Requested Project The below estimate covers time required for design, development, unit testing, and client issue support related to this enhancement. Estimated Cost Solution: Estimated $ WMS TBD SCI LM EEM SO Total: Acknowledgement Your signature is required as proof of acceptance of the extension described above and the estimated cost in order for programming to begin .
